channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Feature Tinubu, fan urges Yoruba quiz Masoyinbo host
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A fan has urged Masoyinbo host Olalekan Fabilola to feature President Bola Tinubu on the popular Yoruba quiz show. Fabilola hints it’s possible.
Read More: https://punchng.com/feature-tinubu-fan-urges-yoruba-quiz-masoyinbo-host/

item.title: Police recover pistol, stolen car in separate Gombe operations
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Gombe State Police Command recovers a Beretta pistol and a stolen Toyota Camry in separate operations, demonstrating effective crime-fighting efforts.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-recover-pistol-stolen-car-in-separate-gombe-operations/

item.title: Singer Niniola resurfaces two months after husband’s death
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Singer Niniola makes her first public appearance two months after her husband’s death, sparking fan concern over her noticeable weight loss.
Read More: https://punchng.com/singer-niniola-resurfaces-two-months-after-husbands-death/

item.title: Foundation Awards Scholarships To 140 Indigent Students In Imo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Fr Mazi UC Foundation, has awarded scholarships to 140 indigent students from Umuezegwu Autonomous Community in Ihitte/Uboma local government area of Imo state to improve the welfare of vulnerable individuals and families. An outreach spearheaded by Nigerian-Austrian Catholic priest, Rev. Fr Hen

item.title: Tinubu Is Nigeria’s Lee Kuan Yew – Uzodimma
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Imo State Governor, Hope Uzodimma has said that Nigeria got another Lee Kuan Yew in President Bola Tinubu, commending the president for his economic reforms, and expressing confidence that his performance would give him a second term in 2027. Uzodimma made the remarks on Friday during a state dinner

item.title: FG clarifies position on raw cocoa exports
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Clarification follows public discussions suggesting Federal Government planned to ban raw cocoa exports after President Bola Tinubu unveiled plans to expand domestic cocoa processing. The post FG clarifies position on raw cocoa exports appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Okocha, Portable step up training ahead of July 31 rematch
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Celebrity actor and boxer Charles Okocha has stepped up preparations for his highly anticipated rematch against street-hop singer Habeeb "Portable" Badmus by training alongside unbeaten heavyweight sensation Moses Itauma ahead of their July 31 showdown in Lagos. The post Okocha, Portable step up tra

item.title: World Cup final: Lionel Messi’s emotional message to Argentina fans, teammates, coaching staff
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, has shared an emotional message to his teammates, coaching staff, and supporters on the eve of their World Cup final with Spain. Messi, on his official Instagram account, also reflected on his years with the national team and the bond formed within the squad leading

item.title: Police Rescue 9 Trafficked Children, Arrest 2 Suspects In Niger
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Operatives of Niger state Police Command have rescued 9 children trafficked in two separate incidents and arrested two suspects in connection with the crimes. LEADERSHIP Sunday gathered that Police operatives attached to Madalla Division while on patrol along Madalla-Suleja Road intercepted and arre

item.title: Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Sunday
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings you the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers on Sunday morning. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Sunday appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Fubara Commiserates With Amaechi Over Mother’s Demise
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Siminalayi Fubara, has commiserated with a former governor of the state, Hon. Chibuike Rotimi Amaechi, on the transition of his mother, Dame Ezinne Mary Oduah Amaechi. In a condolence message issued on behalf of the government and people of the state, Fubara expressed deep sym

item.title: Boniface scores twice as Leverkusen begin pre-season with 7-0 win
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Boniface stole the spotlight in the second half, finding the net twice within five minutes. The post Boniface scores twice as Leverkusen begin pre-season with 7-0 win appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Yobe State Ministry of Health and Human Services has concluded its 2026 Joint Annual Review (JAR) and Quarterly Performance Dialogue, reaffirming the state’s commitment to strengthening the healthcare system through evidence-based planning, collaboration and accountability. item.title: World Cup final: Mbappe makes prediction after France’s defeat to England
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: France captain, Kylian Mbappe, has predicted that Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, will score when Lionel Scaloni’s side face Spain in the World Cup final on Sunday. Mbappe spoke at his post-match interview after France’s World Cup third-place final defeat to England on Saturda

item.title: Spotlight on How THISWEEK Changed the Rules of Nigerian Journalism

item.description: In an era shaped by military rule, fierce newsroom rivalry and constant threats to press freedom, THISWEEK was more than a news magazine; it became a defining force in the

item.title: World Cup: They’ll easily win – Ronaldo predicts Spain vs Argentina final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Brazilian legend, Ronaldo Nazario, has predicted that Spain will easily beat Argentina in Sunday’s World Cup final. Speaking on his official TikTok account with fellow Brazilian Denilson, Ronaldo was asked to predict the World Cup final. The former Real Madrid and Inter Milan star said, &

item.title: Mary Habila’s Death: Activist Petitions IGP, Demands Probe
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Kogi-born political activist and public commentator, Usman Okai Austin, has petitioned the Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Olatunji Rilwan Disu, demanding what he described as an immediate, transparent and comprehensive investigation into the death of 26-year-old Mary Habila. In the petition o

item.title: 3 Killed, Homes Destroyed As Heavy Rainstorm Hits Yobe
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: At least three people have lost their lives, while houses and properties worth millions of naira were destroyed following a heavy rainstorm accompanied by strong winds that struck parts of Nguru Local Government Area in Yobe State. The incident, which occurred overnight, affected Old Nguru, Filing I

item.title: LP, APC clash in Abia as opposition accuses Gov Otti of underpaying workers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The All Progressive Congress, APC, has written to Governor Alex Otti of Abia State, requesting him to improve the living conditions of workers and pensioners in the State. In the said letter, the APC claimed that some civil servants in the State were receiving far below the approved N70,000 national

item.title: Reserved Seats Bill Will Correct Political Imbalance Against Women – Group
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Gender Strategy Advancement International (GSAI) has said increasing women’s participation in Nigerian politics will require deliberate structural reforms rather than symbolic gestures. Speaking with journalists in Abuja, the Executive Director of GSAI, Adaora Onyechere Sydney-Jack, maintained t

item.title: World Cup: Mbappe overtakes Messi in race for Golden Boot ahead of final [Top 7]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: France captain, Kylian Mbappe, has now opened a lead in the race to win the Golden Boot at the 2026 World Cup. Mbappe scored a brace as Les Bleus lost 6-4 to England in the third-place play-off on Saturday night. It means the 27-year-old finished the tournament with 10 goals and four assis

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Presidency has alleged that a rivalry between the Nigerian Army and the Nigerian Air Force during former President Muhammadu Buhari’s administration undermined counterterrorism operations, with troops allegedly denied crucial air support during some military missions. item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England beat France 6-4 in a thrilling third-place match at the 2026 World Cup on Saturday night. The Three Lions raced into a four-goal lead in the first half of the match. Declan Rice opened the scoring, before Ezri Konsa headed in a second and Bukayo Saka scored a brace. But Les Bleus c

item.title: Anambra men get death penalty for rape, murder of 17-year-old
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Two men in Anambra State have been sentenced to death by hanging for the rape and murder of 17-year-old Jennifer Anene. Justice served in this tragic case.
Read More: https://punchng.com/anambra-men-get-death-penalty-for-rape-murder-of-17-year-old/

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina play Spain in the final of the 2026 World Cup, today, Sunday. It is a game that has a lot of subplots. The most obvious is Lionel Messi vs Lamine Yamal. About 19 years ago, Messi, who was a highly-rated youngster at Barcelona, was photographed bathing an infant Yamal. Now, they a

item.title: 2027: ADC raises alarm over removal of billboard in Ibadan
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Gubernatorial candidate of the African Democratic Congress, ADC, ahead of 2027 general elections in Oyo State, Chief Adegboyega Adegoke has raised an alarm over the removal of one of his billboards in Ibadan.  Adegoke made the claim in a statement issued by his media office on Saturday.&#1

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Spain midfielder, Rodri, could become the 10th player to win the World Cup, Ballon d’Or and Champions League. item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US deaths in Jordan attack
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: US forces target Iranian sites for the eighth night in a row, while Iran says it fired drones at the US military in Kuwait.

item.title: Middle East live: US strikes target Revolutionary Guard to 'punish' Iran for killed troops
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Washington launched its eighth consecutive wave of strikes in Iran overnight Saturday with US Central Command saying the latest attacks had targeted Iran's Revolutionary Guard in retaliation for Tehran's killing of two US troops at an airbase in Jordan. A third US soldier is missing in action. Follo

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after two American soldiers were killed by Iranian strikes in Jordan.

item.description: Big Brother Naija Season 6 star Tsakute Ladi Jonah, popularly known as Saskay, has completed her Master’s degree in Global Security and Strategy at the Brussels School of Governance, sharing a look at her 10,329-word thesis on algorithmic counter-terrorism. item.title: ICPC secures final forfeiture of N941m linked to IPPIS fraud
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Investigations revealed that fictitious IPPIS identities had been created for non-existent personnel across multiple MDAs, with salaries paid over extended periods into accounts belonging to individuals and companies. In many cases, the account names did not correspond with those of the purported em

item.title: Watford Accept Aris Bid For Dele-Bashiru
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Greek Super League club Aris are on the verge of completing the signing of Watford midfielder Tom Dele-Bashiru. The two clubs have reportedly reached an agreement on a transfer fee of £425,000 for the 26-year-old, paving the way for his move to Greece. Dele-Bashiru is expected to travel to Greece on

item.title: India’s Private Lenders Bet on Corporate Loan Revival for Growth
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: India’s top private banks are betting on a sustained pick up in lending after reporting healthy numbers in the first quarter of the fiscal year, as more companies shift away from pricier bond market borrowings to cheaper loans.

item.title: Jingye Says It Reserves Legal Rights in UK Steel Nationalization
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Jingye Steel Co. has reserved all legal rights, including international arbitration, over the UK government’s nationalization of British Steel.

item.title: At least one killed as Russian ballistic missiles hit Kyiv
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Russian missiles struck Kyiv and the surrounding region early on Sunday, killing at least one person and injuring nine.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: BUT whilst Britain was contemplating ‘ultimate withdrawal’ from West Africa, France was frantically busy trying to extend the spheres of her influence there. The post The People’s Republic: How they came appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: US Declares Global Crackdown on Left-Wing Extremism, Conflicts with Iran Deepen
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: *Iran will teach US unforgettable lessons, says Supreme Leader Sunday Ehigiator The United States has announced a global campaign against what it described as transnational left-wing extremism, even as its

item.title: ‘Spanish style’ wins over New York’s die-hard World Cup fans ahead of final
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: La Roja's World Cup success has built a new coalition of loyal and recently converted Spanish fans across New York City.

item.title: South Korea to Ease FX Rules for Foreigners to Trade Won Easier
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the forex market and bring the currency closer to full convertibility.

item.title: Nigeria’s Petrol Import Rises 207% to 543m Litres in One Month, Puts Pressure on Naira
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Emmanuel Addeh in Abuja Nigeria’s petrol import increased significantly from 5.9 million litres per day in May to 18.1 million litres per day in June, representing a 207 per cent

item.title: Palestinian teenage footballer dies after Israeli settler attack
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Palestinian teenage footballer, Fadi al-Naasan, has died a week after being shot by Israeli forces.

item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://calculus.academa.ai/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48964585 Points: 28 # Comments: 25

item.title: Navigating the identity and self-worth in the persona of the public administrator
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: ONE of the staples of philosophical ruminations that possesses universal significance is the subject of identity. When the philosopher asks, who are you, she is asking for the mapping of the boundaries of what constitutes the self. The post Navigating the identity and self-worth in the persona of t

item.title: FDA approves new kind of cholesterol pill
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Best England team for a long time? That's a fact, says Rice
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Midfielder Declan Rice says "this is the best England group for a very long time" after the Three Lions secure a third-placed finish at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: When healthcare stops being a promise: AbdulRazaq’a quiet revolution in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Kwara also became the first state in Nigeria to commence payment of the Medical Residency Training Fund in 2025, supporting resident doctors and reinforcing its commitment to developing a sustainable healthcare workforce. The post When healthcare stops being a promise: AbdulRazaq’a quiet revol

item.title: NNPCL’s ₦210 trillion: When facts finally cut through the noise
item.dc:creator: Tribune Opinion
item.description: FOR weeks, the extraordinary figure of ₦210 trillion dominated public discussion around the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited, creating an impression in some quarters that an almost unimaginable sum of public money had simply disappeared. The post NNPCL’s ₦210 trillion: When facts finally

item.title: Andrew Tate and brother arrested in US after more charges laid against them in UK
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are "innocent".

item.title: Tate brothers arrested in Miami as UK adds rape, trafficking charges
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Andrew and his brother Tristan were arrested in the US on Saturday, and the UK is seeking their extradition.
